title: Increase resources for build_each_commit task
body:
The build_each_commit task builds each commit in a pull request
to verify that we have a (at least minimally) functional Podman
at every point, to aid in bisecting.

This task is, right now, extremely slow, taking around 1m40s to
build each commit - which quickly grows unreasonable as PRs grow
to 10+ commits.

Upping resources available to the task should decrease time spent
in CI and reduce the risk of hitting timeouts.

Signed-off-by: Matthew Heon <matthew.heon@pm.me>

Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
LGTM, but definitely want a head nod from @cevich or @edsantiago
If I interpret [this run](https://cirrus-ci.com/task/6221006585724928) correctly, it looks like compiles are down to 35s each. That's still appallingly slow, but half what they were. /lgtm

@edsantiago FWIW: we compromised vs peddle-to-the-metal fast because the vast majority of PRs have only 1 commit, CPU-time is the single most significant expense, and there is a minimum charge for every VM.  What bothers me more finding that 200gig disk reference.  IIRC it was a on a google blog somewhere :confounded: if I can find it now.  Oh well.
